role: Grand Ayatollah; first Secretary of the Guardian Council (1980–1988)
bio: Grand Ayatollah Lotfollah Safi Golpaygani was a senior Shia scholar and one of the longest-lived marjas of the twentieth and twenty-first centuries. He was elected to the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979, representing Markazi Province. Following the constitution's ratification, he was appointed the first Secretary of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council), serving from 1980 to 1988. In this role, he oversaw the council's vetting of legislation for compliance with Islamic law and the constitution during the critical early years of the Islamic Republic. Based in Qom, he was a student of Grand Ayatollah Borujerdi and became a marja' in his own right. He died on 15 January 2022 at the age of 102.
editorial_notes: Born 16 February 1919, died 15 January 2022. Age 102 at death. Guardian Council Secretary 1980–1988. One of the last surviving members of the 1979 constitutional assembly at his death.
